name: numconv

metadata.name: numconv
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Python library to convert strings to numbers and numbers to strings.

documentation:
numconv
=======

numconv is a library that converts strings to numbers and numbers to
strings using default or user supplied encoding alphabets, written by
Gustavo Picón and licensed under the Apache License 2.0.

You can find the documentation in

    https://tabo.pe/projects/numconv/docs/tip/

modules.name: docs.conf
documentation: numconv documentation build configuration file, created by
This file is execfile()d with the current directory set to its containing dir.
Note that not all possible configuration values are present in this
All configuration values have a default; values that are commented out

modules.name: numconv
documentation:
Convert strings to numbers and numbers to strings.

Gustavo Picon
https://tabo.pe/projects/numconv/

classes.name: numconv.NumConv

methods.name: numconv.NumConv.int2str
documentation:
Converts an integer into a string.

```
param num

A numeric value to be converted to another base as a
string.

rtype

string

raise TypeError

when num isn't an integer

raise ValueError

when num isn't positive
```

methods.name: numconv.NumConv.str2int
documentation:
Converts a string into an integer.

If possible, the built-in python conversion will be used for speed
purposes.

```
param num

A string that will be converted to an integer.

rtype

integer

raise ValueError

when num is invalid
```

Class to create converter objects.

```
param radix

The base that will be used in the conversions.
The default value is 10 for decimal conversions.

param alphabet

A string that will be used as a encoding alphabet.
The length of the alphabet can be longer than the radix. In this
case the alphabet will be internally truncated.

The default value is 

raise TypeError

when radix isn't an integer

raise ValueError

when radix is invalid

raise ValueError

when alphabet has duplicated characters
```
